Detailed Description

This class is a factory for creating Connection objects

Definition at line 35 of file Connector.java.

Member Function Documentation

static Connection javax::microedition::io::Connector::open ( String  name  )  throws IllegalArgumentException, ConnectionNotFoundException, IOException, SecurityException [inline, static]

Create a connection object and open it

Parameters:
name A String holding an URL
Returns:
A newly created Connection object
Exceptions:
IllegalArgumentException If the parameter is invalid
ConnectionNotFoundException If the passed name cannot be found or the protocol is unsupported
IOException If any I/O error happens
SecurityException If access to the requested protocol handler is not allowed

Definition at line 74 of file Connector.java.

static Connection javax::microedition::io::Connector::open ( String  name,
int  mode 
) throws IllegalArgumentException, ConnectionNotFoundException, IOException, SecurityException [inline, static]

Create a connection object and open it with the requested access mode

Parameters:
name A String holding an URL
mode The access mode
Returns:
A newly created Connection object
Exceptions:
IllegalArgumentException If the parameter is invalid
ConnectionNotFoundException If the passed name cannot be found or the protocol is unsupported
IOException If any I/O error happens
SecurityException If access to the requested protocol handler is not allowed

References open().

static Connection javax::microedition::io::Connector::open ( String  name,
int  mode,
boolean  timeouts 
) throws IllegalArgumentException, ConnectionNotFoundException, IOException, SecurityException [inline, static]

Create a connection object and open it with the requested access mode and an explicit flag asking (or not) for timeout exceptions

Parameters:
name A String holding an URL
mode The access mode
timeouts True if timeout-related exceptions must be thrown
Returns:
A newly created Connection object
Exceptions:
IllegalArgumentException If the parameter is invalid
ConnectionNotFoundException If the passed name cannot be found or the protocol is unsupported
IOException If any I/O error happens
SecurityException If access to the requested protocol handler is not allowed
